inherited_component: PIS minimizes a path-control KL written as control energy plus a terminal density ratio.
changed_component: SPS minimizes the log ratio between learned forward and unnormalized backward path measures.
claimed_or_observed_effect: The successor retains path-space training while changing the parameterized path pair and objective decomposition.
source_anchor: PIS PDF p. 4, Eq. (13); SPS PDF p. 6, Eq. (2.13)
reasoning: Both equations define a path-measure KL; the compared sources expose the changed factors directly.

inherited_component: PIS learns a forward control on a Brownian reference process.
changed_component: SPS learns forward and auxiliary backward Langevin drifts on a shared trajectory space.
claimed_or_observed_effect: The learned object expands from one controlled forward process to a paired forward/backward construction.
source_anchor: PIS PDF p. 3, Eq. (2); SPS PDF pp. 5-6, Eqs. (2.2)-(2.8)
reasoning: The update equations identify which drift functions are learned and how the two path measures are formed.

inherited_component: PIS corrects finite-control and discretization error with full-trajectory importance weights.
changed_component: SPS applies an independence Metropolis-Hastings accept/reject step using full trajectory probabilities.
claimed_or_observed_effect: The correction changes from a weighted estimator to a target-invariant Markov transition under the stated support condition.
source_anchor: PIS PDF p. 6, Eq. (17); SPS PDF pp. 7-9, Eqs. (2.18)-(2.19)
reasoning: The two correction formulas operate on trajectory likelihood ratios but produce different estimator structures.

inherited_component: The PIS paper names an official public repository that contains training and weighting code.
changed_component: SPS v1 says its code and data will be made public in a future manuscript version.
claimed_or_observed_effect: T4-T5 can be checked on the PIS branch now; the SPS implementation branch must remain unavailable at this cutoff.
source_anchor: PIS PDF p. 9, Sec. 5; SPS PDF p. 21, Sec. 4
reasoning: The source statements determine the executable branch without treating missing code as a scientific result.

inherited_component: PIS tests Bayesian inference, multimodal targets, molecules, and image posteriors.
changed_component: SPS tests two-dimensional lattice phi4 observables and autocorrelation against HMC.
claimed_or_observed_effect: The reported benchmark values cannot be used as a direct performance ranking between PIS and SPS.
source_anchor: PIS PDF pp. 6-9, Sec. 4; SPS PDF pp. 9-20, Sec. 3
reasoning: The tasks, baselines, metrics, and compute accounting differ, so only the mechanism can be compared here.
